message:
feat(ft): wire ft binary to frankenterm-core-replay sub-crate for replay CLI subcommands (ft-y0loj.4 / ft-j1qjt.2 follow-on)

Migrates the four `ft replay …` CLI subcommand handlers in the
frankenterm binary to import their replay types from the new
`frankenterm-core-replay` sub-crate, and adds the regular
(production) dep edge that the new imports require.

Background:
- Commit 9bbb2a60 extracted 24 of 28 replay_* modules from
  `frankenterm-core` into a new sibling crate
  `frankenterm-core-replay`.
- The parent crate's lib.rs no longer re-exports the moved
  modules under `crate::replay_*`. As a result, every consumer
  outside `frankenterm-core` that reaches the moved modules
  via the old `frankenterm_core::replay_*` paths fails to
  build.
- The `ft` binary's `run()` function in
  `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs` is one such consumer,
  hitting the moved modules from inside four replay-CLI match
  arms (Diff, Inspect, plus the Artifact subcommand block, plus
  the report-meta construction inside the Diff arm).

What this commit does:

(a) Workspace dep registration — root Cargo.toml:
    + frankenterm-core-replay = { path = "crates/frankenterm-core-replay",
                                  default-features = false }
    Placed immediately under the existing `frankenterm-core`
    workspace dep entry. The accompanying inline comment spells out
    the dependency-direction invariant: `frankenterm-core` carries
    this sub-crate only as a dev-dep (cycle reason: the
    `crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_replay*.rs` suite needs
    it), but production consumers like `ft` must depend on it
    directly. That's exactly what this commit does.

(b) Binary dep wiring — crates/frankenterm/Cargo.toml:
    + frankenterm-core-replay = { workspace = true,
                                  default-features = false }
    Comment notes the four CLI subcommands in main.rs that drive
    this dep edge (replay diff / inspect / report / artifact-registry).

(c) Cargo.lock catch-up:
    Adds `frankenterm-core-replay` to the resolved `dependencies`
    list of the `frankenterm` package. No new transitive deps are
    pulled in because `frankenterm-core-replay` is already present
    in the lock file from the earlier extraction commit.

(d) Source-side import path migration — crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:
    Six import paths inside the `Replay` command match arms are
    rewritten:
      - replay diff arm:
          frankenterm_core::replay_cli::{DiffRunner, ReplayOutputMode}
            → frankenterm_core_replay::replay_cli::{DiffRunner, ReplayOutputMode}
          frankenterm_core::replay_decision_diff::DiffConfig
            → frankenterm_core_replay::replay_decision_diff::DiffConfig
          frankenterm_core::replay_guardrails_gate::RegressionBudget
            → frankenterm_core_replay::replay_guardrails_gate::RegressionBudget
          frankenterm_core::replay_report::ReportMeta
            → frankenterm_core_replay::replay_report::ReportMeta
      - replay inspect arm:
          frankenterm_core::replay_cli::InspectResult
            → frankenterm_core_replay::replay_cli::InspectResult
      - replay artifact arm:
          frankenterm_core::replay_artifact_registry::{
              ArtifactManifest, ArtifactRegistry,
              ArtifactSensitivityTier, ArtifactStatus,
              ListFilter, PruneOptions
          } → same identifiers, swapped to
              frankenterm_core_replay::replay_artifact_registry::*
    No identifier names, no method calls, no business logic, and
    no error handling change — purely the crate-name half of the
    `use` statements.

Why this couldn't be a re-export shim:
- The whole point of the replay extraction was to break the
  parent crate's dep on those modules so the `ft` build graph
  could skip them when not needed. Adding back a
  `pub use frankenterm_core_replay::*` re-export in
  `frankenterm-core` would re-create the parent-side dep on
  the sub-crate, defeating the extraction. The forward-looking
  fix is that direct consumers depend on the sub-crate
  directly, exactly as this commit does.

Cycle / build-graph properties:
- Production cycle: NONE.
  `frankenterm` → `frankenterm-core-replay` → `frankenterm-core`.
  Forward-only graph; cargo accepts.
- Dev-dep cycle: pre-existing.
  `frankenterm-core` (test target) → `frankenterm-core-replay`
  via dev-dep is the cycle that allowed the extraction in the
  first place. This commit doesn't introduce any new cycle.

Verification expected post-commit:
- `cargo check -p frankenterm` succeeds (was failing prior to
  this commit on the four `frankenterm_core::replay_*` import
  lines).
- `cargo build -p frankenterm` produces the `ft` binary with
  all four replay subcommands functional end-to-end.
- `ft replay diff`, `ft replay inspect`, `ft replay artifact …`
  CLI behavior is unchanged — only the import path moves.

Co-author note: changes authored by parallel agents in this
multi-agent session; committed verbatim per the
"treat parallel-agent changes as your own" rule from AGENTS.md.

files.patch:
@@ -552,6 +552,12 @@ franken-agent-detection = "0.1.0"
 
 # Internal crates
 frankenterm-core = { path = "crates/frankenterm-core", default-features = false }
+# Replay sub-crate extracted from frankenterm-core under ft-y0loj.4 / ft-j1qjt.2.
+# `frankenterm-core` carries this only as a dev-dep (cycle reason: tests in
+# crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_replay*.rs); production consumers
+# such as the `ft` binary that need the moved replay_* modules must depend
+# on this crate directly.
+frankenterm-core-replay = { path = "crates/frankenterm-core-replay", default-features = false }
 frankenterm-alloc = { path = "crates/frankenterm-alloc" }
 
 [profile.release]

@@ -24,6 +24,12 @@ workspace = true
 
 [dependencies]
 frankenterm-core = { workspace = true, default-features = false }
+# ft-y0loj.4 / ft-j1qjt.2: 24 replay_* modules moved out of frankenterm-core
+# to this sub-crate. `ft` consumes a handful of them directly (replay diff /
+# inspect / report / artifact-registry CLI subcommands in main.rs), so we
+# depend on the sub-crate as a regular dep here. `frankenterm-core` itself
+# only dev-depends on this crate, so there's no production cycle.
+frankenterm-core-replay = { workspace = true, default-features = false }
 frankenterm-alloc = { workspace = true, optional = true }
 clap.workspace = true
 chrono.workspace = true
